name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-59
title: session-init.py and refresh.py paths-with-./ handling can resolve to wrong file
description: hooks/session-init.py lines 56-59 and refresh.py lines 97-101: 'full = root / path.lstrip("./")'. str.lstrip('./') treats '.' and '/' as a CHARACTER SET, stripping any leading run of those characters. A path like '....foo.md' becomes 'foo.md' (the leading dot was stripped — fine), but '/etc/passwd' becomes 'etc/passwd' (the absolute path turned into a relative one and resolved under repo_root). The earlier 'if path.startswith("/")' branch handles absolute paths, so the bug doesn't manifest today, but the lstrip pattern is incorrect — should use path.removeprefix('./') (Python 3.9+) or careful conditional stripping. Defense-in-depth concern.

history.note: wave-4 cleanup; fixed in e10610a (impl) and 91f2c68 (tests)
